tables.tableDescription: Consolidated view of ECM (Equity Capital Markets) and DCM (Debt Capital Markets) deals, tranches, orders, and investor allocations. Use PRODUCT column to filter between 'ECM' and 'DCM' records.

columns.columnDescription: Product type identifier. 'ECM' for Equity Capital Markets, 'DCM' for Debt Capital Markets. Use this column to filter by product.
Unique identifier for the deal/transaction. Primary key for linking deals to orders and tranches. Corresponds to dealId in the UI.
The name of the deal/transaction (e.g., syndicate deal name). Displayed in the UI header.

Unique identifier for a tranche within a deal. A deal can have multiple tranches. Used as a foreign key to link orders to specific tranches. Populated for both ECM (O.TRANCHE_ID) and DCM (O.PARENT_ID).
Human-readable name of the tranche. Displayed in the orderbook grid's 'Tranche' column.

Total size of the tranche offering, STORED AS TEXT (VARCHAR2). Always wrap in TO_NUMBER(TRANCHE_SIZE DEFAULT NULL ON CONVERSION ERROR) before comparing, ranking, or summing. For ECM, deal size in shares; for DCM, total Nominal/Principal of the tranche.

The name of the INVESTOR/account that placed the order or received an allocation. Also known as investor or account. This is the human-readable name - for FILTERING/JOINING use GPNUM (the precise investor identifier). In queries, prefer GPNUM for exact investor matching.

Geographic region/country of the INVESTOR (where the investor is based) - e.g. 'Germany', 'United States', 'EU'. DISTINCT from TRANCHE_REGION/DEAL_REGION (the deal's target region NAM/EMEA/APAC). Literals: us/usa/american -> UPPER(INVESTOR_REGION) LIKE '%UNITED STATES%' (NEVER bare '%US%' - matches RUSSIA/AUSTRIA); german -> '%GERMANY%'; eu/european -> = 'EU'. Values unverified - prefer full words with LIKE.
Timestamp when the deal/tranche was priced.
Denomination in which all cashflows associated with the deal are made, also known as pricing currency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

Settlement date. Date on which the transaction is completed, with investors making the payment and issuer delivering the securities. Note: often NULL for DCM rows, but kept available for both products.
Tranche-level geographic region - the default region column for both products. Values: NAM, EMEA, APAC. This is the DEAL/tranche side, never the investor's geography.

Final allocation to the investor. This is the ECM investor-side metric (a SHARE count on ECM). On DCM it is currently mapped from order-amount fields (a money value). 'How much did investor X get/invest' on ECM -> SUM(ALLOCATION). Because the unit differs by product (ECM shares vs DCM money), never SUM ALLOCATION across both products together - GROUP BY PRODUCT or scope to one.

The key/code for the INVESTOR category classification (e.g., LONG_ONLY, HEDGE_FUND, OUTRIGHT). Used for investor summary grouping. INVESTOR-side column - any GROUP BY on this column implies an investor-scoped query; pair with investor-level aggregation.

Classification of the investor. Exact DB values: Outright, Long Only, Hedge Fund, Long/Hedge, Outright/Hedge, Central Bank, Official Institution, Insurance/Pension, Asset Manager, Corporate Treasury, Bank Treasury, Private Bank, Co-lead Retention, Co-lead Trading, Co-lead Order, Co-lead Pot, Other Trading, Broker, Syndicate, JLM Trading, Other. Not valid: Strategic, Family Office, Retail, SWF, DSP, Index, Quant.
Coded roadshow meeting type (ECM). Values: ONE_TO_ONE, CONFERENCE_CALL, SMALL_GROUP, GROUP_MEETING, NO_MEETING. '1x1'/'one-to-one' -> ONE_TO_ONE; 'other than 1x1' -> <> 'ONE_TO_ONE'.

Human-readable meeting type between the issuer/syndicate and the investor (e.g., 'No Meeting', 'One-to-One', 'Conference Call', 'Small Group', 'Group Meeting'). Display counterpart of MEETING_TYPE_KEY.
Name of the issuing company/entity raising capital through the deal.

Credit ratings of the issuer from rating agencies (e.g., Moody's / S&P / Fitch). Primarily populated for DCM records but kept available for both products. Pipe-separated with NO agency key column - the notation identifies the agency (Moody's Aaa/Aa2 vs S&P/Fitch AAA/AA-).
Stock exchange ticker symbol of the issuer. Primarily populated for ECM records.

Industry sector of the issuer. Exact DB values: Aero/Defense, Agriculture, Autos, Banks, Chemical, Consumer Goods, Energy, Financial Services, Healthcare, Industrials, Information Technology, Insurance, Pharmaceuticals, Retail, Telecommunications, Transportation, Technology. Note 'Information Technology' and 'Technology' are distinct, and there is no 'Financials' (use 'Financial Services' or 'Banks').
Stock exchange on which the issuer's securities are listed/traded. Primarily populated for ECM records.

Current lifecycle status of the deal (deal level only). Values seen include Settled, Final Settled, Live, Priced, Draft, Postponed, Announced, Cancelled, Confidential, Deleted, Allocated, Subject, Archived, FreeToTrade, Mandated, Private, Open, Closed. WARNING: casing is inconsistent in the data (both 'Open' and 'OPEN', 'Closed' and 'CLOSED') - always compare with UPPER() on BOTH sides.

Total size of the deal across all tranches. For ECM, expressed in shares or monetary value at deal level; for DCM, total Principal/Nominal amount across tranches. See TRANCHE_SIZE for tranche-level size.
Deal-level geographic region. Values: NAM, EMEA, APAC (there is no 'AMER'). Populated for ECM; use TRANCHE_REGION for tranche-level or generic region asks.
Legacy lineage key (order -> its deal). All deal/tranche/order facts are already flattened onto every row - NEVER join or self-join this view; filter DEAL_ID directly.
Legacy lineage key (order -> its tranche, DCM). Facts are already flattened onto every row - NEVER join; filter TRANCHE_ID directly.

Unique identifier for an individual investor order. DCM ONLY - not usable on ECM rows (ECM grain is DEAL_ID+INVESTOR_NAME). ECM order counts = COUNT(DISTINCT INVESTOR_NAME) per deal, never COUNT(DISTINCT ORDER_ID).

Order HANDLING type. Values include OTT, AWAY, REGULAR (and others; list not exhaustive). 'away orders' / 'orders from other banks' -> ORDER_TYPE LIKE '%AWAY%'. NOTE: LIMIT / MARKET / SCALED are the INDICATION type and now live in IOI_TYPE, not here.

Indication of Interest (IOI) type - how the indication is priced/structured. Values: LIMIT, MARKET, SCALED (not exhaustive). Distinct from ORDER_TYPE (handling: OTT/AWAY/REGULAR). 'limit/market/scaled orders' -> IOI_TYPE (UPPER LIKE). BUT 'got scaled back'/'scaled-down allocation' = an allocation cut - rank by DEMAND_QTY - ALLOCATION, NOT this column.

Demand quantity - shares/amount the investor asked for (indication), before allocation. ECM and DCM. 'Demand'/'indication in shares' -> DEMAND_QTY (never ALLOCATION or AMT). Compare with ALLOCATION for the final approved amount.

Order/indication MONEY amount. This is the DCM investor-side money metric. For ECM rows AMT is generally empty/zero - use ALLOCATION for ECM investor amounts. 'How much did investor X invest/order': ECM -> SUM(ALLOCATION), DCM -> SUM(AMT). Never SUM(AMT) on an ECM-scoped query.

Type of the offering. ECM values are ONLY 'IPO' and 'FO' (follow-on). 'Convertible', 'Block' and 'Rights Issue' are NOT offering types - convertibles live in EQUITY_TYPE. DCM: benchmark or tap issuance.

Fine-grained ECM security type (more specific than EQUITY_TYPE): e.g. ADR, ADS, GDR, GDS, Common Stock, Rights, Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock. Free text - prefer LIKE. Use EQUITY_TYPE for coarse security class; both columns can contain 'Common Stock'.

Bond/product class. Exact DB values: Investment Grade, Preferred, Emerging Market, Covered Bond, High Yield, Agencies, CLO, LevFin Loan, Asset Backed, SSA, Taxable Muni, ABS, RMBS, CMBS, Municipals. Expand IG->'Investment Grade', HY->'High Yield', EM->'Emerging Market' (never the abbreviation itself). Mainly DCM.

Type of equity offered. Exact DB values: 'Equity Units', 'Exchangable Notes' (sic - misspelled in the data), 'Global Depository', 'Convertible Bonds', 'Common Stock', 'Convertible Preferred', 'Warrants'. NOTE: the value is 'Common Stock', NOT 'Common Shares'. For 'convertible(s)' use LIKE '%Convertible%'. ECM rows.
Indicates whether the bond is an ESG-labelled instrument (e.g., Green, Social, Sustainability, Sustainability-Linked). Only applies to DCM records.
Stated purpose for which the issuer will use the funds raised (e.g., General Corporate Purposes, Refinancing, Acquisition, Green Projects).

Tenor(s) of the tranche - time to maturity labels. FORMAT DRIFTS: '2Y','2-Y','2M','2-M' (hyphen optional; Y=years, M=months). Pipe-delimited list, position-aligned with SECURITIES_MATURITY. Match format-insensitively with a digit boundary: REGEXP_LIKE(TENORS, '(^|[^0-9])2[- ]?Y', 'i') - never = '2Y' (misses 2-Y) and never bare LIKE '%2Y%' (false-matches 12Y). DCM rows.
Maturity date(s) of the securities issued in the tranche. Only applies to DCM records. Pipe-delimited, position-aligned with TENORS.
Ranking of the debt within the issuer's capital structure (e.g., Senior Unsecured, Senior Secured, Subordinated, Tier 2). Only applies to DCM records.

Coupon structure of the debt instrument. Exact DB values (note SPACES, not hyphens): 'Fixed', 'FRN', 'Zero Coupon', 'Fixed to FRN', 'Fixed to Fixed', 'Exchanged', 'Structured', 'Funged', 'Step Coupon'. Map floating->'FRN', fixed-to-floating->'Fixed to FRN'. DCM rows.
Frequency of coupon payments (e.g., Annual, Semi-Annual, Quarterly). Only applies to DCM records.

Regulatory registration category. Values include 144A, Reg S, private placement, eurobond, domestic - match with LIKE. Bare '144A' / 'Reg S' / '3(a)(2)' asks -> THIS column; only '<x> delivery' wording -> DELIVERY_TYPE. DCM rows.

Name of the syndicate bank/member on the deal. ECM = pipe-delimited (' | ') aligned list of full bank names; DCM = a single bank string which IS the bill-and-deliver (B&D) bank. Match a bank by LIKE on its stem (e.g. '%Citigroup Global Markets%'), not exact equality.

Role of the syndicate member on the deal (e.g. Lead Manager/Bookrunner, Joint Bookrunner, Passive Bookrunner, Co-Manager, Selling Group, Bill and Deliver, Global Coordinator). ECM only: pipe-delimited (' | ') aligned list. NULL on DCM rows.
Code identifying the broker on the order. ECM only: pipe-delimited (' | ') aligned list of broker codes. NULL on DCM rows. Citi codes include CITIDEV, CITIUSA, CITIAUS, CITIASIA, CITIUKE, CITGMCA.

Bill-and-Deliver (B&D) flag for the broker/syndicate member on the order. Populated for BOTH products but in different shapes: ECM = pipe-delimited list aligned position-by-position with BROKER_CODE/SYNDICATE_MEMBER_NAME (never compare the whole field with '='; use token-aware REGEXP with the ' | ' boundary); DCM = single scalar boolean-like value. TRUE tokens: true/t/yes/y/1. B&D filters must also include BROKER_CODE or SYNDICATE_MEMBER_NAME context.
Type of security identifier in IDENTIFIER_VALUE. Exact DB values: 'CUSIP', 'FIGI', 'Valoren', 'ISIN', 'RIC', 'SMCP ID'.
Value of the security identifier (e.g., the ISIN or CUSIP code) for the tranche/security.

Execution stage of the deal (Live/Priced/Executed/Closed/Cancelled). Overlaps DEAL_STATUS - use ONLY when the user explicitly says 'execution status'; any generic 'status / live / priced / closed deals' ask -> DEAL_STATUS.

Global Financial Client ID - identifies the ISSUER (the company raising capital) on the deal. Use for ALL issuer-side filters, e.g. WHERE GFCID = '<issuer-gfcid>' after resolving the issuer name. NEVER use GFCID for investor filters - investors are filtered by GPNUM.
Legal delivery type: '144A', 'RegS', '3(a)(2) Exempt' only. Use ONLY when the user says 'delivery' - bare 144A/RegS asks go to REG_CATEGORY. DCM rows.

Global Participant Number - identifies the INVESTOR (the buyer / allocatee / order-placer) on the deal. Use this for ALL investor-side filters, e.g. `WHERE GPNUM = '<investor-gpnum>'` or `WHERE GPNUM IN ('<gp1>', '<gp2>')`.

relationships.note: Flattened view - all deal/tranche/order facts are on every row. Do NOT join; filter DEAL_ID / TRANCHE_ID / ORDER_ID directly.
